info.title: lifx
info.description:
LIFX CLI - Control your LIFX smart lights from the command line
Features:
- Cloud API: Control lights via internet (requires API token)
- LAN Protocol: Control lights on local network (no API token needed)
- Secure credential storage via Windows Credential Manager
Quick Start (Cloud):
1. Get API token: https://cloud.lifx.com/settings
2. Store token: lifx cloud key set <token>
3. Control lights: lifx cloud lights on all
Quick Start (LAN - no token needed):
lifx lan discover

options.description: Enable verbose output with detailed information

options.name: --token
options.description: LIFX Cloud API token (overrides stored credential)

options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: cloud
commands.description:
Control LIFX lights via Cloud API (requires API token)
The Cloud API allows you to control your LIFX lights from anywhere with internet.
Requires an API token from https://cloud.lifx.com/settings

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output with detailed information

options.name: --token
options.description: LIFX Cloud API token (overrides stored credential)

commands.name: key
commands.description:
Manage LIFX Cloud API token (securely stored)
The API token is required for all Cloud API features.
Storage method depends on your platform:
- Windows: Encrypted using Data Protection API (DPAPI)
- Linux/macOS: Stored in ~/.lifx/credentials.json with 600 permissions
Get your API token:
1. Visit https://cloud.lifx.com/settings
2. Log in to your LIFX account
3. Click 'Generate New Token'
4. Copy the token and store it using: lifx cloud key set <token>
Security Note:
- Never share your API token
- Token is encrypted (Windows) or protected by file permissions (Unix)
- Clear your command history after setting token
